steps.title: Spot the subject and the key agreement signal
steps.content:
The grammatical subject is “Neuros Medical's technology” (singular).

The phrase “alongside other such neuromodulation technologies” is parenthetical, set off by commas.

The preposition “alongside” signals that the verb agrees with the main subject alone, not with “technologies.”

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement to the verb choices
steps.content:
”Is paving” correctly uses the singular auxiliary verb with the present participle to form a complete verb phrase. A is only a participle without an auxiliary (incomplete); C and D use plural auxiliaries.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
